title: BLUE MOUNTAIN COMMUNITY COLLEGE - 211info
text: BLUE MOUNTAIN COMMUNITY COLLEGE BLUE MOUNTAIN COMMUNITY COLLEGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers community college education including university transfer degrees, career and technical education certificates and degrees, adult basic skills and GED preparation, college readiness, workforce training, continuing and community education, and lifelong learning opportunities. The Adult Basic Education (ABE) program is for individuals with or without a high school diploma or GED to help prepare for college-level coursework, advance in their careers, or achieve personal milestones by strengthening essential reading, writing, and math. Campuses are located in Pendleton (main campus), Hermiston, Milton-Freewater, Baker City, Boardman, La Grande, and John Day. 2411 NW Carden Avenue Pendleton, OR 97801 Eligibility: Must be age 16 and not enrolled in school. Students under age 18 must have a release from school district in which they reside before attempting to pass the GED test. Hours: Schedule varies Email (541) 276-1260 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 276-1260 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Walk in or call for more information or appointment FEES: ABE, GED, and ELA Program Fee (Title II): $34 fee per term. $9 for each additional college prep course per term. Financial assistance available. DOCUMENTS: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: State of Oregon Services: Adult Basic Education Community Colleges GED/High School Equivalency Test Instruction Other Services Offered At This Location: EDUCATIONAL PROGRAMS - ESL AND EL CIVICS FOOD - THE FOOD DEN If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
